findings.description: The source unit bundles two distinct directives: (1) person-first/audience-specific terminology rules and (2) a procedural requirement to 'confirm audience in every brief before writing.' The candidate rule only captures the terminology guidance and drops the brief-confirmation obligation, which is a separate actionable governance step and should be split into its own rule.

new_rule.statement: Confirm audience (patient/DTC vs HCP) in every brief before writing.

findings.description: The rule states a compliance-relevant terminology distinction (patient vs HCP language) but omits the governance facet of who/when verifies audience alignment (the 'confirm audience in every brief' step), leaving the rule's enforcement mechanism unspecified.

payload.governance_facet: audience must be confirmed in brief prior to copy drafting

findings.description: Hardness is set to strong_default, but the source language ('master reference ... across all LISRAYA materials') asserts an absolute, universally scoped governance role for the patient brochure with no exception language, suggesting hard_constraint is more accurate.

Class 'assembly' mischaracterizes this rule; the source describes a content-governance/master-reference authority relationship rather than a structural assembly instruction. A class like 'governance' or 'content_sourcing' would better reflect the rule's intent.
